Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results state PTST is encoded by locus At5g39790, and Materials list ptst-1 as SALK_025022 and ptst-2 as FLAG_622E03.

Source_Species.reason: The study is on Arabidopsis PTST mutants and Arabidopsis PTST protein.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Materials specify A. thaliana plants; this supports Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: ptst-1 is in Columbia/Col background and ptst-2 is in Wassilewskija/Ws background; Col-0 is also used in tables/materials.

Applied_Species.reason: Experiments were performed in Arabidopsis and transient localization assays were performed in tobacco leaves.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana and Nicotiana benthamiana are both explicitly named in Materials/Results.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Columbia/Col-0 and Ws are explicit for Arabidopsis, but no tobacco variety/cultivar is given; the field appears incomplete for all applied species.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: PTST was detected in Arabidopsis leaf extracts, and transiently expressed PTST-YFP in tobacco localized to the plastid/chloroplast stroma.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper repeatedly shows PTST is required for amylose synthesis and ptst mutants are amylose-free.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Amylose is explicitly described as a glucose polymer component of starch, i.e. a polysaccharide.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Abstract and introduction state amylose strongly influences physicochemical behavior of starchy foods during cooking and non-food manufacturing uses.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The work concerns Arabidopsis leaf starch granules in chloroplasts, where amylose is synthesized/accumulates.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Abstract/Results describe PTST as a plastidial protein with an N-terminal coiled coil domain and a C-terminal carbohydrate binding module; Results specifically identify a CBM48 domain.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Results show most PTST is in the soluble fraction and PTST-YFP is stromal; a small amount is granule-bound in wild type.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: In vitro pull-down and in planta immunoprecipitation show PTST physically interacts with GBSS.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are present in Results: T-DNA mutants, iodine colorimetry, Sepharose CL-2B, granule-bound immunoblotting, soluble/insoluble fractionation, starch-binding assay, tobacco confocal localization, GBSS-GFP overexpression, in vitro pull-down, and in planta co-IP.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show PTST is required for normal amylose synthesis and GBSS targeting; ptst mutants produce amylose-free starch with unaltered total starch.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each numbered claim is supported by Results/Table 1/Figs. 3, 6, 7, 8, and qPCR in S2 Data; the ~100-fold reduction is explicitly referenced from S3 Fig.

Other_Important_Info.reason: PTST being conserved and single copy in Arabidopsis is in Fig. 1 text; W217A/W255A abolishing starch binding and preventing GBSS localization is shown in Figs. 7 and 8B; ptst sex4 intermediate amylose accumulation is supported by Table 2/Fig. 4.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion explicitly proposes PTST as a promising biotechnology target to reduce amylose without yield penalty and suggests PTST overexpression, especially with GBSS, may increase amylose.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Table 1 shows no total starch penalty in Arabidopsis leaves; Discussion states crop responses may differ and prolonged starch synthesis may permit some amylose accumulation.

Omics_Data.reason: Phylogenetic analysis, TreeBASE study 16699, rice Genevestigator expression (S8 Fig.), and qPCR of GBSS transcripts in ptst (S2 Data) are all explicitly mentioned.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Plants were grown under a 12-h light/12-h dark cycle, and Results discuss leaf age effects in ptst sex4 due to prolonged starch retention.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Results/Methods state 35S promoter was used for GBSS-GFP overexpression and Discussion mentions 35S-driven transient overexpression context in tobacco.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: PTST protein was analyzed in Arabidopsis leaf extracts, and Discussion cites rice PTST expression in leaf and developing endosperm from S8 Fig.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Materials give the gbss mutant line GABI_914G01, but the locus At1g32900 is not stated in the paper text provided.

Enzyme_Name.reason: GBSS is explicitly defined as GRANULE-BOUND STARCH SYNTHASE.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is given anywhere in the paper text.

Source_Species.reason: The studied GBSS is Arabidopsis GBSS.

Applied_Species.reason: Most direct mutant/overexpression analyses are in Arabidopsis, but important localization/co-IP assays for Arabidopsis GBSS were also performed in tobacco leaves; restricting applied species to Arabidopsis omits part of the Results.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is correct for several experiments, but Arabidopsis GBSS was also tested in Nicotiana benthamiana transient assays.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The gbss mutant and transgenic GBSS-GFP lines were in Columbia background.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results state GBSS transcript expression is maximal 2 h into the light period and experiments concern leaf tissue.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: GBSS is studied in the context of starch metabolism/biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper explicitly states GBSS is specifically responsible for amylose synthesis.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Discussion states GBSS buried within the granule processively elongates linear chains; this is effectively the terminal polymer elongation step for amylose within the granule.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Discussion and in-gel assay state GBSS uses ADP-Glc/ADP-glucose, and dpe1 discussion supports glucan primers/malto-oligosaccharides for GBSS activity.

Primary_Product.reason: The paper repeatedly states GBSS is specifically responsible for amylose synthesis.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This description matches statements that GBSS uses ADP-Glc to processively elongate linear chains within starch granules to make amylose.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: GBSS is presented as the enzyme directly responsible for amylose biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Amylose is the direct product linked to GBSS in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Amylose is described as a glucose polymer/polysaccharide.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Abstract explains amylose determines starch physicochemical behavior relevant to cooking and industrial processing.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The study analyzes amylose in Arabidopsis leaf chloroplast starch granules.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: GBSS is a starch synthase, and Results predict a coiled coil in the C-terminal region.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: GBSS is exclusively starch-bound in wild type; when not efficiently targeted it remains stromal in tobacco and largely soluble when overexpressed in ptst Arabidopsis.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Direct interaction with PTST is shown by pull-down and co-immunoprecipitation.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed approaches are present in the Results, including mutant analysis, immunoblotting, tobacco localization, GBSS-GFP overexpression, pull-down, co-IP, in-gel activity assay, and amylose measurements.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Discussion explicitly states previous work suggests GBSS does not catalyse the rate-limiting step in amylose synthesis.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: gbss mutants are shown/used as amylose-free controls and produce amylose-free starch.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary statements are supported by the Results and Discussion, including the ~100-fold reduction in ptst and direct PTST interaction/localization requirement.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Arabidopsis GBSS requiring Arabidopsis PTST in tobacco is shown in Fig. 8; instability/degradation of free GBSS is proposed from Results; K441E/E452K and E486A effects are shown in Fig. 6.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion explicitly says GBSS is the prior direct amylose-control target and notes previous overexpression in potato and wheat did not increase amylose; PTST stacking is proposed.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Discussion supports that overexpression alone may not increase amylose and Results/Discussion support instability of free GBSS.

Omics_Data.reason: qPCR of GBSS transcripts in ptst (S2 Data) is supported, but the statement 'no accession provided' is metadata not a paper finding; also this field is mixed in scope.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Plants were grown under a 12-h/12-h cycle and starch was analyzed at end of day/night in leaves.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 35S promoter is explicitly stated for GBSS-GFP overexpression in Arabidopsis; Discussion also refers to 35S promoter in transient tobacco overexpression context.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The Arabidopsis GBSS mutant/background studied is Columbia/Col-0.

corrections.reason: Materials give the gbss mutant line GABI_914G01, but the locus At1g32900 is not stated in the paper text provided.
No EC number is given anywhere in the paper text.
